steps.title: Fit the opening phrase
steps.content: The blank begins the introductory phrase before the comma. Because the painting was completed in the past, the phrase needs a past participle that can modify her painting without creating a separate clause.

steps.title: Reject finite verbs
steps.content: Choice A is future tense, and Choice C is present tense; neither can start this introductory participial phrase. Choice D is a finite passive verb, so it creates a comma splice before the main clause.

steps.title: Use the participle
steps.content: Choice B forms the smooth participial phrase Completed in 1557, which correctly modifies the painting before the main clause says where it can now be found. The answer is B.
